metadata.title: Wide Figures, Equations, and Tables in IEEE Format
metadata.abstract: This fixture tests the wide-element rendering pipeline: wide figures that span both columns, numbered display equations rendered via the matplotlib mathtext backend, and wide tables. The document also includes inline equations within paragraph text and a full references section.

blocks.text: Consider a linear system described by the state-space representation shown in equation [EQ 1]. The system matrix A governs the dynamics while B represents the input matrix.
The output equation is given by [EQ 2], where C is the observation matrix and D is the feed-through matrix.
Figure 1 shows the system architecture overview. This figure spans the full page width for clarity.

blocks.caption: System architecture overview spanning both columns for improved readability.

blocks.text: The performance metrics are summarized in Table I below, which also spans the full page width.

blocks.caption: Comparative performance metrics across baseline, proposed, and optimized configurations.

blocks.text: We have demonstrated wide figures, display equations, and wide tables. The continuous section breaks correctly switch between single-column and two-column layouts without page breaks.

references.title: A New Approach to Linear Filtering and Prediction Problems
